actions.label: Hire a Chief Revenue Officer
actions.description: Bring on a seasoned CRO to own growth. They don't just advise — every month they execute the highest-leverage marketing move for you automatically, so you stop running the funnel by hand. A real salary, but you buy back your time.
actions.lesson: The point of a business is to make it run without you. A great executive hire is the ultimate leverage: you trade salary for freedom and the business keeps growing while you work on it, not in it.
actions.narrative_success: Your new CRO is in the chair. From now on Marketing runs itself — each month they pick and execute the smartest growth play while you focus on capital and lifestyle. Their pay scales with the company (a market salary plus performance bonuses).
actions.narrative_failure: The CRO is hired but ramping slowly — the first month was mostly onboarding. They'll be picking your growth moves from here. Their pay scales with the company.

actions.label: Refine Your Offer
actions.description: Stop selling time. Package your expertise into a clear, premium offer with defined scope and pricing — so every customer is worth more.
actions.lesson: Sell outcomes, not hours — to people who can pay. A sharper, premium offer raises the value of every single customer you have, which is far more powerful than just adding more of them.
actions.narrative_success: The new pricing feels scary — and every new prospect is saying yes faster than before. You were undercharging.
actions.narrative_failure: Two clients pushed back on the new pricing. You compromised on one. The packaging needs another pass.

actions.label: PR & Brand Campaign
actions.description: Invest in a real brand push — podcast appearances, press features, a professional brand refresh. Authority lets you charge more.
actions.lesson: Authority is pricing power. When prospects already know your name from a podcast or feature, you're not discounting to win them — they expect to pay a premium, and every customer is worth more.
actions.narrative_success: A local business podcast featured you. Three inbound calls the next week, all mentioning the episode — and none of them flinched at your rates.
actions.narrative_failure: The rebrand looks great. The press pitch didn't land. Brand equity went up; the phone didn't ring more. Yet.

actions.label: Build a Content Engine
actions.description: Hire a content team. Blog, video, podcast — all running without you. The brand grows on autopilot, lifting what every customer is worth.
actions.lesson: A content team makes your brand compound on autopilot. A bigger brand means higher perceived value — you raise prices and capacity without trading more of your hours for attention.
actions.narrative_success: The content calendar runs itself. You approved this month's posts in twenty minutes. Your brand — and your pricing power — keeps climbing.
actions.narrative_failure: The team is producing content. It's... fine. Not your voice yet. Takes time to train a brand voice into other people.

actions.label: License or Franchise
actions.description: Package your business model for others to run. Your brand, their sweat. Massively expands how much value your name can capture.
actions.lesson: Sell the system, not your time. Licensing your playbook lets others run your model under your brand — multiplying the value your business can capture far beyond what your own delivery could ever reach.
actions.narrative_success: Two licensees signed. They're paying you monthly for the right to use your name and your playbook. This is leverage.
actions.narrative_failure: One licensee signed, then struggled. Supporting them took more time than expected. The model needs refinement.

actions.label: Cold Outreach Blitz
actions.description: Spend the month calling, emailing, and DM-ing everyone who might need what you sell. Unglamorous, but it fills the top of the funnel.
actions.lesson: Early on, volume wins. Talk to enough of the right people and the math produces leads. Don't polish the funnel before you've filled it.
actions.narrative_success: Forty calls, a dozen real conversations — and your pipeline has more names in it than it's ever had.
actions.narrative_failure: Forty calls, twelve conversations, one maybe. But that one maybe is a lead you didn't have before.

actions.label: Local Networking
actions.description: Hit every chamber event, BNI meeting, and coffee meetup in your area. Shake hands until your palm hurts.
actions.lesson: In-person is the highest-trust top-of-funnel there is. People remember someone they've met — these become the warmest leads you'll work all year.
actions.narrative_success: You're becoming a familiar face. Two people approached you this time instead of the other way around.
actions.narrative_failure: A lot of business cards exchanged, no deals closed. But three people now know your name.

actions.label: Social Media Content
actions.description: Post consistently about your work — case studies, tips, behind-the-scenes. Build an audience one post at a time.
actions.lesson: Organic content compounds slowly but never stops. It won't pay this month — it builds an audience that feeds leads into your funnel for years.
actions.narrative_success: One post went semi-viral in your niche. Your DMs have actual prospects in them for the first time.
actions.narrative_failure: Consistent posting, crickets responding. But the archive is building — it'll compound eventually.

actions.label: Test Paid Ads
actions.description: Run a small Facebook or Google ads test. Burn a little cash to learn what message lands and turn it into leads.
actions.lesson: Master one channel at a time. Paid ads give the fastest feedback and the fastest leads — pour in there first, build organic in parallel for the long game.
actions.narrative_success: The ads are profitable. Not wildly — but the cost per lead is low enough to pour more in. You've found a lever.
actions.narrative_failure: The $500 mostly evaporated. But you learned what doesn't work, which is worth something.

actions.label: Create a Lead Magnet
actions.description: Free guide, checklist, or mini-course that captures emails. Give value first, sell later.
actions.lesson: Give value before you ask for the sale. A free, genuinely useful resource earns trust and an email address — the start of a lead you own.
actions.narrative_success: The lead magnet is live. Fifteen new emails in the first week. These aren't customers yet — but they raised their hand.
actions.narrative_failure: Downloads are trickling in. The topic might need adjusting, but you've got five new leads and a distribution channel.

actions.label: Ask for Referrals
actions.description: Systematically ask every past and current client for warm introductions. Costs nothing but ego.
actions.lesson: Referrals are the cheapest, highest-trust leads you'll ever get. Happy clients are a sales force you don't pay — but only if you actually ask.
actions.narrative_success: Your best client introduced you to three people at her networking group. Warm leads, already half-sold.
actions.narrative_failure: Everyone said they'd think about it. One person actually came through — better than nothing.

actions.label: Build Referral Partnerships
actions.description: Set up formal referral agreements with complementary businesses. Their clients become your leads.
actions.lesson: Borrow other people's trust. Partnering with businesses that serve your same client (but don't compete) turns their audience into a steady, near-free lead source.
actions.narrative_success: Three partners, each sending you one to two warm leads a month. The acquisition cost on these is nearly zero.
actions.narrative_failure: One partnership is producing. The other two signed the agreement but haven't sent anyone yet. Follow up next month.

actions.label: Build JV/Affiliate Network
actions.description: Create a network of affiliates and joint-venture partners who send you a flood of leads on commission.
actions.lesson: An affiliate network is an army feeding your funnel on commission — you only pay for results. Other people's audiences become your lead channel, with almost no downside risk.
actions.narrative_success: Twelve affiliates are actively promoting. Leads from channels you didn't build yourself. This is what scale feels like.
actions.narrative_failure: The network exists on paper. Three affiliates are actually producing. The rest need nurturing.

actions.label: Launch a Webinar Funnel
actions.description: Build an automated webinar that sells while you sleep. High setup cost, compounding conversion.
actions.lesson: Build it once, it sells forever. An automated funnel turns your best pitch into an asset that converts leads 24/7 — lifting your close rate without costing you time.
actions.narrative_success: The webinar converted at 8%. People are signing up, watching, and buying — at 2 AM while you're asleep.
actions.narrative_failure: Registration was decent. Conversion was terrible. The offer needs reworking, but the funnel infrastructure is built.

actions.label: Hire a Salesperson
actions.description: Bring on a dedicated closer. Your lead-to-customer conversion climbs and you stop being the bottleneck — but their pay scales with the business, so the cost grows as you do.
actions.lesson: A dedicated closer lifts conversion on the leads you already have — more customers from the same pipeline. But sales talent is paid on results: their cost rises as revenue rises, so the real leverage comes from a better product and a warm pipeline, not just adding salespeople.
actions.narrative_success: Your new closer is converting leads you'd have let go cold. Conversion is up — and you spent the month on the business instead of in it.
actions.narrative_failure: The new hire is ramping slowly. Conversion hasn't moved much yet, but you got ten hours a week back.

actions.label: Install CRM & Pipeline
actions.description: Stop tracking leads in your head. Build a real sales pipeline with stages, follow-ups, and metrics so fewer leads slip away.
actions.lesson: What gets tracked gets closed. A real pipeline surfaces the leads you'd otherwise forget and lifts your conversion — most lost sales die from neglect, not rejection.
actions.narrative_success: For the first time, you can see every prospect, where they are, and what happens next. Three forgotten leads turned into deals.
actions.narrative_failure: The CRM is set up but half your data is still in sticky notes. Partial improvement — you'll migrate the rest next month.

actions.label: Launch Email Nurture Campaign
actions.description: Build an email list from your leads. Weekly value emails that warm them up until they convert.
actions.lesson: An email list is an asset YOU own — not rented from an algorithm. Nurture it and cold leads warm into buyers, lifting conversion on the pipeline you already paid to fill.
actions.narrative_success: The email sequence is converting. Three leads became clients this month — they said your emails made them feel like they already knew you.
actions.narrative_failure: Open rates are decent, clicks are low. The content needs work, but the infrastructure is built.
